subject: hid-microsoft: add XOne Elite 2 ID.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Xbox Elite 2 controller (0x045e:0x0b22) would not be recognized for force-feedback rumble support in 5.15 fork kernel

notes: Fork commit (March 2022) preempted vanilla's formalization by ~13 months. Vanilla 6.18.38 (April 2023, commit f5554725f30475b05b5178b998366f11ecb50c7f) adds the same device (0x045e:0x0b22) with identical driver_data (MS_QUIRK_FF) but uses different #define naming: USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_CONTROLLER_MODEL_1797_BLE instead of fork's USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_ELITE2_CONTROLLER. This is not a backport; the fork originated the support and vanilla later added a more comprehensive approach (also covering old PID 0x0b05 for model 1797, and separately fixing model 1708's firmware-update PID break — neither part of this fork commit). No userspace coupling: Main_MiSTer does not reference the #define symbols or the raw ID 0x045e:0x0b22 anywhere (grep of Main_MiSTer/*.cpp,*.h for "0b22"/"0B22" returns nothing). Note this is the Bluetooth-mode PID (0x0b22); Main_MiSTer's XBox-Elite-2 paddle-shift quirk (QUIRK_SHIFT, input.cpp:5523/5810) is keyed on the DIFFERENT wired/dongle PID 0x0b00 and its own source comment states it 'works only through cable or USB dongle' — confirming it does not apply to this BT-mode ID, corroborating the coupled=false finding here (see sonnet-verified sibling record 409f81077926d4a0e2c0cc7ac11dda7605f79469 where that coupling DOES apply). Not carried as patch in our 6.18 build; vanilla provision is sufficient. Failure is silent: dropping this from fork 5.15 would cause Xbox Elite 2 rumble to cease working over Bluetooth without boot/build errors. Distinguish from: commit 9a8cb6a93 (Xbox Series X/S controller at 0x0B13, Sept 2021) and commit c784a6856 (Elite 2 paddle buttons support, April 2026). sonnet-verified: confirmed disposition/equivalence/device-ID/userspace-coupling and vanilla-SHA ancestry; corrected vanilla_file_line — hid-ids.h:903 does not match the live v6.18.38 checkout (MODEL_1797_BLE is actually defined at hid-ids.h:1013; hid-microsoft.c:458-459 was already correct).
